info.description: This page documents the REST APIs that perform Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager operations.
info.title: Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager API Reference

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: If present, get host groups from this domain. Else get host groups from logged in domain.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

401.description: The user that is currently logged on has insufficient rights to execute the web method, or the user is unauthorized.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

410.description: Can not find the object specified

500.description: The web service encountered an error while processing the web request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the policy to get.

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: The type of the policy to get.

200.description: Web service is processed successfully, result is returned

401.description: The current logged in user does not have sufficient rights to execute the web method or user is unauthorized

404.description: The resource reguested was not found

500.description: The web service encountered an error processing the web request

get.summary: Get Policy XML for specified policy id. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: If present, get policies from this domain. Else get policies from logged in domain.

get.summary: Get the policy summary for specified policy type. Also gets the list of groups to which the policies are assigned.

get.operationId: Get Policy Summary with Policy Type

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: If present, get policies from this domain. Else get policies from logged in domain.

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Get summary for all the policies of this type.

get.summary: Get the policy summary for specified policy type. Also gets the list of groups to which the policies are assigned.

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

company.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The name of the company that published the object.

directory.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The directory where the object was originally found.

name.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The name of the file object; for example, iexplore.exe

sha2.description: The SHA-256, SHA-1, or MD5 checksum of the object content.

size.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The size of the object, in bytes; for example, 69472.

action.description: The action of the threat in exceptions policy.

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

applicationcontrol.description: Exclude this file from application control

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

path.description: The path to the object.  Relative path if pathvariable is defined.  Full path if pathvariable is [NONE] or not specified

pathvariable.description: A prefix variable indicates a well-known Windows folder

securityrisk.description: Exclude this file from security risk

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

enabled.description: Ability to disable a rule temporarily from enforcement without deleting it

source.description: Which 3rd-party management system was the source of the rule; ex, SEP, ATP

delete_unused_content_after.description: Time of deleting unused content after it

name.description: The name of the threat, as identified by the specific definition engine.

state.description: Indicates whether the technique is enabled or disabled.

schedule.description: Download schedule configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy
